statement.markdown: This problem requires you to implement a function that finds the maximum contiguous subarray sum of an array of integers. The function should take an array of integers as input and return the maximum subarray sum. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that prevents it from returning the correct result for certain inputs.
statement.notes: The function should handle arrays with both positive and negative integers. Time complexity should be O(n), where n is the length of the input array.

constraints.description: The length of the input array

examples.explanation: The maximum subarray sum is 7, which is obtained from the subarray [4, -1, -2, 1, 5].
The maximum subarray sum is 15, which is obtained from the subarray [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].
The maximum subarray sum is -1, which is obtained from the subarray [-1].

hints.text: Check the implementation of Kadane's algorithm.

editorial.title: Editorial: Finding the Maximum Subarray Sum
editorial.intuition: The function should keep track of the maximum subarray sum and the current subarray sum. It should update the maximum subarray sum whenever the current subarray sum becomes greater than the maximum subarray sum.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try all possible subarrays and return the maximum sum.
editorial.betterSolution: Kadane's algorithm: Keep track of the maximum subarray sum and the current subarray sum.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Implement Kadane's algorithm with a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Proof of correctness: The optimal solution is correct because it implements Kadane's algorithm, which has a time complexity of O(n).
commonMistakes: Failing to update the maximum subarray sum.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int maxSubArray(vector<int>& nums) { int n = nums.size(); int max_sum = INT_MIN; int current_sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { current_sum += nums[i]; max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum); if (current_sum < 0) { current_sum = 0; } } return max_sum; } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public int maxSubArray(int[] nums) { int n = nums.length; int max_sum = Integer.MIN_VALUE; int current_sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { current_sum += nums[i]; max_sum = Math.max(max_sum, current_sum); if (current_sum < 0) { current_sum = 0; } } return max_sum; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def maxSubArray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int: n = len(nums) max_sum = float('-inf') current_sum = 0 for i in range(n): current_sum += nums[i] max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum) if current_sum < 0: current_sum = 0 return max_sum

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int maxSubArray(vector<int>& nums) { int n = nums.size(); int max_sum = INT_MIN; int current_sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { current_sum += nums[i]; max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum); if (current_sum <= 0) { max_sum = current_sum; current_sum = 0; } } return max_sum; } };
